etymology.description: 春(春) (chūn, "spring") refers to the season following winter, characterized by new growth and renewal. 天(天) (tiān, "sky; day") can mean the sky or represent the broader context of a day, often symbolizing an overarching period of time.
etymology.interpretation: 春(春) (chūn, "spring") is the season when nature revitalizes after winter, with a burst of life and growth. 天(天) (tiān, "day") relates to the overall span or time frame during which these transformations occur. Together, 春天 (chūntiān, "spring") encapsulates the notion of the spring season as a period marked by rejuvenation and renewal, governed by the conditions and influences of both earth and sky.

examples.chinese: 春天到来时(春天到來時)，鸟儿开始歌唱(鳥兒開始歌唱)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān dàolái shí, niǎoér kāishǐ gēchàng.
examples.english: When spring arrives, the birds begin to sing.

examples.chinese: 春天是个美丽的季节(春天是個美麗的季節)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān shì gè měilì de jìjié.

examples.chinese: 在春天(在春天)，花儿绽放出新芽(花兒綻放出新芽)。
examples.pinyin: Zài chūntiān, huāér zhànfàng chū xīnyá.
examples.english: In spring, flowers bloom with new buds.
